summary: Restore a welcome sample with its renderable path, migrate scratch autosave atomically, quote the installed Windows file-association command, and bind Linux window evidence to the exact AppImage child runtime.

externalInputs.purpose: Observe the exact-candidate production-static reload, Windows file-association, and Linux AppImage window-evidence failures and retain their hosted evidence.

decisions.decision: Persist scratch recovery as a strictly validated version-2 snapshot containing both project-relative path and source. Read legacy version-1 source as Untitled.scad, write version 2 atomically through one storage value, and remove version 1 only after the new value is stored.
decisions.rationale: Source-only recovery reconstructed a welcome sample as extensionless Untitled, changing the engine entry file after restart. The path is part of the render snapshot identity and must survive with the bytes. A renderable legacy fallback preserves old profiles without inventing a historical path that was never stored.

decisions.decision: Persist a changed clean primary-scratch identity immediately while retaining the configured debounce for dirty edits. Keep additional scratch tabs explicitly unpersisted and clear their warning whenever the active state no longer violates that rule.
decisions.rationale: Opening a clean built-in sample changes the path and source without making the document dirty. Waiting only for dirty edits omitted that state from recovery; applying the edit debounce to a clean sample also left a reload race. Immediate identity persistence is bounded and does not mark user edits saved.

decisions.decision: Use the current-user NSIS post-install hook to rewrite the OpenSCAD model ProgID open command as an exactly quoted installed executable followed by an exactly quoted %1 argument. Preserve the existing backup, restore, and uninstall behavior.
decisions.rationale: The generated association quoted the document argument but not the executable path. That failed the literal installed lifecycle and would parse incorrectly when the install path contains spaces.

decisions.decision: Treat APPIMAGE_EXTRACT_AND_RUN as a supervising launcher. Prove the visible application by recursively locating a descendant whose executable hash equals the extracted embedded ScadMill runtime, then require an exact-title visible positive-geometry X11 window owned by that same PID using xdotool --all semantics. Isolate and clean up the process group and retain bounded timeout diagnostics.
decisions.rationale: The prior check hashed and queried the supervisor PID even though the GUI runs as a child, and xdotool's default predicate combination was not conjunctive. Binding artifact hash, descendant runtime, PID, title, visibility, and geometry prevents a different process or window from satisfying the proof.

testEvidence.criterion: A scratch document restores the same renderable entry path and source, including a clean welcome sample and legacy version-1 data.
testEvidence.test: Focused adapter, application, autosave, packaged-evidence, and production-static recovery coverage.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/m3-production-static.e2e.ts --config playwright.production-static.config.ts; pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-web/browser-project-metadata.test.ts tests/ui/Workbench.test.tsx tests/ui/files/ScratchAutosave.test.tsx tests/scripts/packaged-desktop-evidence.test.ts -t 'migrates legacy|routes every global File shortcut|keeps an additional scratch tab|validates the versioned scratch snapshot'
red.outcome: RED: the reloaded sample rendered as Untitled and failed; the strengthened focused contracts then reported four missing migration, packaged-evidence, save-contract, and warning-transition behaviors.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/platform-web/browser-project-metadata.test.ts tests/ui/files/ScratchAutosave.test.tsx tests/ui/files/use-file-commands.test.tsx tests/app/App.test.tsx tests/scripts/packaged-desktop-evidence.test.ts -t 'browser project metadata persistence|debounces a dirty scratch|keeps an additional scratch|persists a clean welcome|useFileCommands|restores the scratch entry path|validates the versioned scratch snapshot'; pnpm.cmd exec playwright test tests/e2e/m3-production-static.e2e.ts --config playwright.production-static.config.ts
green.outcome: GREEN: 16/16 focused unit/integration assertions passed and the production-static cached reload journey passed 1/1 with gear_knob.scad restored and rendered.

testEvidence.criterion: The installed Windows .scad command quotes both the exact installed executable and the file argument.
testEvidence.test: Static NSIS hook contract plus the retained hosted installed-lifecycle assertion.

red.outcome: RED: the new exact post-install command assertion failed because NSIS_HOOK_POSTINSTALL was absent; hosted run 29554621985 independently failed the installed ProgID command assertion.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/windows-installer.test.ts -t 'produces an offline current-user NSIS installer'
green.outcome: FOCUSED GREEN / HOSTED REPLACEMENT PENDING: 1/1 selected contract passed; the next exact-candidate installer run must prove the registry value end to end.

testEvidence.criterion: Linux lifecycle evidence binds the exact AppImage to its exact child GUI runtime and that runtime's visible ScadMill window.
testEvidence.test: Fail-closed workflow contract, exact extracted shell syntax check, and hosted AppImage lifecycle replacement.

red.outcome: RED: the strengthened workflow contract failed on missing recursive descendant proof; hosted run 29554621985 timed out while checking the supervising AppImage PID.

green.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'),yaml=require('yaml'); const w=yaml.parse(fs.readFileSync('.github/workflows/ci.yml','utf8')); process.stdout.write(w.jobs['desktop-installers'].steps.find(s=>s.name==='Launch and remove Linux AppImage candidate').run)" | & 'C:\Program Files\Git\bin\bash.exe' -n
green.outcome: FOCUSED GREEN / HOSTED REPLACEMENT PENDING: the exact YAML step parsed with bash -n and exited 0 with no diagnostics; the focused workflow contracts independently passed 3/3. The next hosted AppImage run remains the runtime proof.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
